title: cirsium eriophorum var. richterianum
intro: Cirsium eriophorum var. richterianum, also known as the woolly thistle, is a variant of the woolly thistle plant species, characterized by its distinctive white or light-colored hairs that cover its leaves and stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The woolly thistle has been valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, as well as its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to potential interactions or side effects.
